scope: Calculate engine torque from measured power and RPM with reference verification and tolerance analysis.

decision_context.system_boundary: Engine Torque Calculator for automotive service, workshop, or field estimation.
decision_context.single_operation_scope: Compute torque from power and speed using the standard torque equation T = (P × 9550) / N.
decision_context.decision_owner: technician, mechanic, estimator, fleet operator, or checker
decision_context.decision_after_output: Screening torque value based on verified power, RPM, and reference tolerances.
decision_context.cost_of_wrong_decision: Wrong inputs or misunderstood output can cause incorrect torque estimation, equipment mismatch, or unsafe operation.
decision_context.failure_mode_if_formula_wrong: Incorrect source values or formula binding can understate torque, leading to underspecified components.
field_use_limitations: Decision support only. Verify high-risk applications through qualified review before commitment.

calculation_basis.public_summary: The public result is based on normalized user inputs, validation blockers, evidence confidence, sensitivity ranking, and public-safe output redaction.

inputs.name: Engine Power (measured)

physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_min: Engine Power cannot be below the lower physical bound.
physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_max: No universal upper bound is enforced; review source evidence.

engineering_range.source: Typical engine power range for passenger and commercial vehicles.

reference_values.source: Engine specification sheet or manufacturer data plate.
reference_values.public_note: Reference values are advisory and must not auto-fill critical inputs.

evidence_requirement.public_help_text: Use measured or source-backed values where available. Estimated critical values reduce confidence.

inputs.user_help_text: Enter the verified engine power output for this job context.
inputs.warning_if_missing_or_estimated: Engine Power is a decision-sensitive input. Missing or estimated values move the result to review.

physical_hard_bounds.semantic_error_message_max: No universal upper bound is enforced.

reference_values.source: Engine specification sheet or tachometer reading.
reference_values.public_note: Reference values are advisory and must not auto-fill critical inputs.

evidence_requirement.public_help_text: Use measured or source-backed RPM values where available.

inputs.user_help_text: Enter the verified engine speed in RPM for this job context.
inputs.warning_if_missing_or_estimated: Engine Speed is a decision-sensitive input. Missing or estimated values move the result to review.

rules.semantic_message: Correct physically impossible inputs before calculation.
rules.why_it_matters: Invalid source values can create unsafe outputs.
rules.suggested_action: Verify measurement, unit selection, and source evidence.

rules.condition_description: Critical input evidence is missing or estimated.
rules.semantic_message: Critical input evidence is missing. Treat the result as review-only.
rules.why_it_matters: Evidence quality can flip the decision.
rules.suggested_action: Attach source record or mark the value as verified.

rules.semantic_message: Non-finite numeric values are not allowed.
rules.why_it_matters: NaN or infinite outputs cannot support audit-ready decisions.
rules.suggested_action: Correct the input values and units.

rules.condition_description: Selected unit is missing or unsupported.
rules.semantic_message: Select a supported unit for every required input.
rules.why_it_matters: Formula execution requires normalized base-unit values.

formulas.name: Engine Torque server-side deterministic formula
assumptions: Inputs are user-verified job, asset, or manufacturer values.
The server formula module owns exact deterministic calculation logic.
Public outputs are decision-support estimates, not certification or legal proof.
limitations: This tool does not replace qualified engineering review for safety-critical work.
Reference ranges are advisory and never override measured source data.
Exact formula expression is restricted to server-side execution files.
edge_cases: Missing evidence moves the result to REVIEW.
Non-finite or physically impossible inputs block execution.
formulas.public_proof_summary: The result is derived from user-entered technical inputs after unit normalization, bound checks, evidence review, and public-safe redaction.
formulas.protected_methodology_summary: Server-only deterministic logic computes torque from power and RPM using the standard torque-speed relationship without exposing exact formula expressions.
formulas.checker_note: Review normalized inputs, evidence status, and output sensitivity before making a commitment.
formulas.acceptance_logic: Proceed only when primary output, evidence confidence, and sensitivity review support the decision.
formulas.rejection_logic: Reject when the primary output indicates an unsafe or physically implausible condition.
formulas.review_required_logic: Review is required when evidence is missing or a critical input is estimated.
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Server-side uncertainty model ranks input sensitivity and flags decision-threshold crossings.

outputs.name: Engine Torque
outputs.acceptance_threshold: Tool-specific review thresholds are enforced server-side when source data is sufficient.
outputs.review_threshold: Review is required when evidence is missing or confidence is low.
outputs.failure_threshold: Block only on impossible input or non-finite output.
outputs.public_explanation: Use this output as a screening value for engine torque.
outputs.operator_explanation: Use this output to evaluate the engine torque output.
outputs.engineer_explanation: Review source inputs, normalized units, and sensitivity before committing.
outputs.owner_cfo_explanation: Use the result as a technical screening value.
outputs.checker_explanation: Check source evidence, normalized units, and audit seal before relying on the result.
outputs.hidden_risk_note: Low-quality source inputs can flip the decision even when the numeric output appears acceptable.
outputs.suggested_action: Proceed only if the result remains acceptable after evidence review and sensitivity checks.

fields.primary_reason: Decision status is generated server-side from primary output, evidence confidence, validation blockers, and sensitivity.

user_profile_summary.operator: Use the output to decide whether the engine torque is within acceptable range.
user_profile_summary.engineer: Check normalized inputs and sensitivity before relying on the result.
user_profile_summary.checker_auditor: Verify evidence, audit seal, redaction status, and public proof pack.

hidden_risk_explanations: Estimated or missing critical inputs can flip the result.
next_best_actions: Proceed, reject, or escalate according to status.

states.label: REVIEW
states.technical_message: Review required due to evidence, range, or sensitivity condition.

states.label: WARNING
states.technical_message: Result is close to a technical decision threshold.

states.label: CRITICAL
states.technical_message: High exposure or unsafe condition requires escalation.

proof_pack.purpose: Generate review-ready engineering evidence for technical checking and shop-floor documentation.
public_report_rules: Do not expose exact formula expressions.
Show source context, use scope, limitation, evidence status, protected methodology, result interpretation, uncertainty, and audit seal.

audit_trail_contract.tamper_warning: Changing any input, unit, schema version, formula version, or output invalidates the audit seal.

input_groups.title: Reference Values (Optional)
input_groups.description: Optional reference values for tolerance comparison.

test_plan: reference values are visible but do not autofill critical inputs

metadata.change_log_summary: Initial Free Tool V5.3.1 schema for Engine Torque Calculator.
